dialogue.utterance: Unit 4 is dispatched to 917 Evergreen for a seizure event.
Hi, I'm Jordan, an EMT with the rescue squad. What led you to call 911 today?
My husband was having a seizure. He fell and there was no response.
Sir, can you open your eyes for me?
Do you know your name and the date today?
My name is Darrel. I don't know the date.
Can you lift your left arm for me?

10. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs and symptoms; EMT: Are you having a headache or dizziness now, sir? 11. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: signs and symptoms; Patient: No, I feel better now, but I don't remember what happened.
Have you had seizures before? 13. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: pertinent past medical history; Bystander: Yes, back in January. He stopped taking medication called Keppra.

Would you mind sharing your husband's full medical history? 15. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: pertinent past medical history; Bystander: He has epilepsy. He also has a pacemaker due to bradycardia.
Last meal or drink before the seizure? Was it today?
I don't know exactly, but it was before he went to the restroom.

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: What happened just before the seizure started?

dialogue.micro_intent: events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: He was standing and then suddenly yelled. I heard him fall.
Mr. Jackson, can you speak comfortably?
Your breathing is steady - within normal limits.
Your pulse is strong and consistent. Skin is warm and dry. No signs of being pale or diaphoretic.
I'm going to check your head for swelling. Do you mind?
No visible head trauma, but there's slight blood-tinged sputum at the corner of the lip.
Performing a 4-lead ECG now.
Sinus rhythm. No ECG emergencies detected.
Do you mind if I take a 12-lead as well? This will take a few seconds.
12-lead EKG: sinus rhythm with possible PJC bigeminy.
Pulse is 108. Fast for you, sir.
Respirations are at 16 per minute - within normal range.
Blood pressure is 99/57 - on the low side.
Spo2 is 97%, normal for now.
Blood glucose is 105 - that's good.
We're placing an IV for future medications if needed. Your left hand?
20-G IV in the left hand now.
We're administering 5 mg of Versed IV now. It may help with any further seizures.
Your GCS is 14. Your eye and motor responses were good, but you were somewhat confused verbally.
Blood glucose is still stable at 105. No changes.
Sir, you appear to be in a postictal state with altered mental status - A.M.S. You're alert but confused.
Pulse is now at 105-slightly decreased.
Blood pressure risen to 123/74. That's better.
Respirations now at 16 per minute - still normal.
12-lead EKG repeated. Bigeminy pattern remains, no new findings.

Have there been any stress tests or neuro exams in the past? 45. History of Present Illness; S.A.M.P.L.E.: pertinent past medical history; Bystander: Yes, there were some in the past with no recurrence of seizures since January.
Does your husband have a known cardiac reason for his seizures?
Yes, they think the pacemaker was placed due to his hypotension and bradycardia.
We'll be transporting to St. Mary's as they specialize in cardiac and neurological emergencies.
Can we assist you to the stretcher? You'll be more comfortable lying down.
Transitioning from floor to stretcher with a secure hold and pivot method.
All straps and rails secured for your comfort and safety.
Sir, you'll be transported to the ER as quickly and safely as possible.
Blood pressure is 130/70. That's a positive change.
Respirations at 20 per minute - mildly increased, but still within range.
Pulse is 89, decreased further now.
Spo2 is 99%, now normal.
Blood glucose remains at 105 - stable for now.
Another 12-lead confirms sinus rhythm; bigeminy persists but no new findings.
No changes noted in respiratory rate or effort.
Spo2 remains at 99% - continuing without concern.
GCS has improved to 15. Verbal confusion resolving.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo SAMPLE - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: I understand your husband was told the pacemaker is due to heart issues - is that related to his seizure triggers?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo SAMPLE - pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: The doctor mentioned hypotension and a likely link to seizure triggers.
When did the seizure end? Has your husband been cleared of altered mental status?
Five minutes ago - he was up, but remains a bit confused.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo SAMPLE - events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: It's good to know there was nothing in the intake or other symptoms as part of the event.
Last meal or drink - when was that?
He had a large dinner two hours ago but no drink he could recall.
Your condition is trending stable now. We've got consistent vitals on board.
No changes noted - blood pressure is still 130/70.
Pulse steady at 89.
Minor variation in respiratory rate - at 20 - still within expected range.
Blood glucose remains 105 - nothing alarming.
Hold on tight - we're doing a proper secure pivot and will be in the truck shortly.
You're very welcome on board. Let's keep this ride as smooth as possible.
We're just one block away from the ER - they have care ready.
Everything continues to look stable. We'll keep monitoring closely.
Blood pressure is consistent at 130/70.
Respirations at 19 per minute now.
Pulse remains at 89. No bradycardia noted.
Blood sugar remains stable. Everything looks good so far.
Oxygen saturation is 99%, and stable. No desaturation observed.
Any changes in how you're feeling since the Versed was administered?
No pain - I'm feeling clearer now.
On a scale of 0 to 10 now?
Still 0.
Blood sugar still holds steady at 105 - not a concern.
No additional meds required at this point - will continue to observe for further sequelae.
IV remains patent - no flushing or need for further lines.
We're 90 seconds from arrival - you're doing really well.
19 per minute. No worsening, no signs of distress.
129/69 - we're showing slight but normal variation.
99% - only marginally declined but within safe range.
St. Mary's is expecting one seizure patient with stable vitals - all set for triage.
All set for stabilization in the truck - equipment is hooked and go for immediate triage.
ED will have your husband stable in a hall for further medical evaluation - we'll give any updates if he had a repeat episode during transport.
All straps and support in place. Ready for arrival.
No notable variation; 130/70.
Normal rhythmic breathing, no signs of distress.
Still stable 89 - good to go.
Going back up to 99% from 97% - positive change.
Since the start, how long was the seizure episode?
Just about 2 minutes - no prolonged convulsions noted.
Any meds or drinks since the episode ended?
No - he has been sedated post-Versed, no intake.

dialogue.micro_intent: redo SAMPLE pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: I've noted no new medical concerns. Any updates in medical history to note before arrival?

dialogue.micro_intent: redo SAMPLE pertinent past medical history
dialogue.utterance: No, it hasn't changed from what I've mentioned - his meds were Keppra but discontinued.
Transitioning to all clear for ED arrival - vitals normal now.
Any further complaints or changes in altered mental status after the episode?
No - he's clear now, though a bit disoriented.
ED will manage remaining protocols - cardiac and neurological evaluation based on EKG and initial findings.
